project.title: JackLaw Streamlined Trigger → Rule / Procedure → Action Master List
project.purpose: Capture Jack’s chronological litigation knowledge with the fewest fields necessary before a separate developer-recognition mapping phase.
project.recognition_layer: Deferred. Search terms, aliases, OCR cues, extraction fields, and channel-specific detection are preserved as background and will be finalized with the developer.
global_standards: Use a short chronological trigger name in ordinary litigation parlance.
The trigger is the operative event, not merely the email, scan, upload, call, or outgoing document carrying it.
State the hard legal/court rule and the JackLaw procedure together in one field, clearly labeled when both exist.
Create one separately typed action for each calendar entry, document, communication, filing/service step, list update, review, follow-up, vendor step, or close condition.
Calendar actions contain the literal desired calendar wording.
Document actions identify the exact draft or output to generate.
Case/List Update actions identify the exact system, project, list, PHS, or status record to update.
Current primary authority and controlling court/provider requirements must be verified before programming mandatory legal logic.

records.trigger: A new lead or prospective-client contact is received.

exclusions: A source channel by itself is not the trigger; the content must evidence this event.
Do not duplicate the same operative event merely because it is forwarded, re-uploaded, or received through several channels.
Do not finalize a deadline until the operative date, service method, and controlling document are verified.
branch_questions: Does a more specific trigger apply?
What facts determine the rule branch?
Does human confirmation precede calculation?
completion_evidence: Source linked; required outputs completed or affirmatively closed; reviewer and timestamp recorded.

records.trigger: Initial screening information is complete enough for conflict, limitations, and viability review.
records.rule_procedure:
JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Screening target: same day or within 24 hours.
• Calculate limitations immediately.
• Create internal warnings 60 and 30 days before earliest confirmed deadline.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• What is the earliest plausible deadline?
• Is an administrative prerequisite required?
• Is emergency filing/preservation needed?
• Accept, investigate, refer, or decline?

authority.confidence: Strong firm source; law is claim-specific

records.trigger: A judge, department, courthouse, or direct-calendar assignment notice is received.
records.rule_procedure:
LEGAL / EXTERNAL RULE
• CCP 170.6 and current assignment-specific authority; do not encode one universal deadline.

JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Research judge and department immediately.
• Internal source uses a 10-day shorthand; verify before programming.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• When is assignment deemed made?
• Which CCP 170.6 timing branch applies?
• Keep or challenge judge?
• Any standing order?

records.trigger: A defendant is served with the summons and complaint.
records.rule_procedure:
LEGAL / EXTERNAL RULE
• Trigger-specific service and response rules; verify method and effectiveness.
• Internal shorthand: personal response +30; substituted commonly +40 total — verify.

JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Obtain and review proof of service before final calculations.
• Create answer/default and plaintiff-discovery-control dates after method verification.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• Personal or substituted?
• Entity agent valid?
• Mailing completed?
• Service contested?
• When legally effective?

records.trigger: The response deadline expires without a responsive pleading or approved extension.
records.rule_procedure:
LEGAL / EXTERNAL RULE
• Current default statutes, rules, local forms, service validity, and damages requirements.

JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Docket-check before default.
• Internal calendar source proposes default-request follow-up 10 days after response expiry; confirm.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• Truly no response?
• Default timing?
• Damages adequately pleaded?
• Amend complaint first?

records.trigger: A notice setting the initial Case Management Conference is received.
records.rule_procedure:
LEGAL / EXTERNAL RULE
• Meet and confer: proposed 30 calendar days before CMC; verify current CRC 3.724.
• CM-110: proposed 15 calendar days before CMC; verify current CRC 3.725.
• Backward counting and noncourt-day handling require verified date-engine rules.

JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Calendar hearing plus one-week tickler.
• Calendar CM-110 deadline plus one-week tickler.
• Calendar meet-and-confer deadline plus one-week tickler.
• Proposed: generate/send M&C request 2 calendar days before legal deadline — confirm.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• Initial or continued CMC?
• Any OSC?
• Department-specific requirements?
• All parties served/at issue?

actions.text: [CASE SHORT NAME] – Initial Case Management Conference – [CMC DATE] at [CMC TIME] – Dept. [DEPARTMENT] – [COURTHOUSE / APPEARANCE METHOD]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – 1 WEEK TICKLER: Initial CMC on [CMC DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – LAST DAY to file and serve Case Management Statement (CM-110) for CMC on [CMC DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – 1 WEEK TICKLER: CM-110 due [CMC STATEMENT DUE DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – LAST DAY to meet and confer with opposing counsel regarding CMC on [CMC DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – 1 WEEK TICKLER: CMC meet and confer due [CMC M&C DUE DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – Send CMC meet-and-confer request/letter to [OPPOSING COUNSEL] for CMC on [CMC DATE]
CMC meet-and-confer request: exact subject/body and proposed-time language require Jack approval.
Retrieve current CM-110, notice, docket, department rules, and best approved samples.
Create seven separately linked calendar actions.
Recalculate dependent dates if CMC changes.
Attorney approves CM-110 and M&C positions.

records.notes:

records.trigger: The firm serves written discovery or a deposition notice.
records.rule_procedure:
LEGAL / EXTERNAL RULE
• Current Civil Discovery Act, plaintiff hold rules, numerical limits/declarations, deposition rules, and service extensions.

JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Serve discovery at first opportunity.
• Wave 1 candidate: FI General + Employment, RFP, PMQ notice, pin-down letter.
• Set PMQ 6–8 weeks out; create 14-day follow-up.
• Wave 2 candidate: SROGs, RFAs, FI Set 2; declaration if required.

records.trigger: Written discovery, a subpoena, or a deposition notice is served on the firm or client.
records.rule_procedure:
LEGAL / EXTERNAL RULE
• Interrogatories: proposed 30 days plus authorized service extension; verify current CCP 2030.260.
• RFP/inspection demands: proposed 30 days plus authorized service extension; verify current CCP 2031.260.
• RFAs: proposed 30 days plus authorized service extension; verify current CCP 2033.250.
• Electronic service must be modeled as a separate +2 court-day adjustment where authorized, not a blind 32-calendar-day offset.
• Signature and verification requirements vary by discovery type.

JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Create a separate package for each instrument and set.
• Two-week tickler 14 calendar days before due date.
• One-week tickler 7 calendar days before due date.
• Complete client discovery call within first 7 calendar days.
• Open response shells, questionnaire, document list, verifications, and workflow same day.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• Which separate instruments/sets?
• Valid proof of service?
• Personal/electronic/mail/overnight?
• Shortened time/order?
• Subpoena/deposition branch?

actions.text: [CASE SHORT NAME] – LAST DAY for [RESPONDING PARTY] to serve responses to [PROPOUNDING PARTY]’s [DISCOVERY TYPE], Set [SET NUMBER]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – 2 WEEK TICKLER: [RESPONDING PARTY]’s responses to [DISCOVERY TYPE], Set [SET NUMBER], due [RESPONSE DUE DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – 1 WEEK TICKLER: [RESPONDING PARTY]’s responses to [DISCOVERY TYPE], Set [SET NUMBER], due [RESPONSE DUE DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – COMPLETE BY: client discovery call for [DISCOVERY TYPE], Set [SET NUMBER], due [RESPONSE DUE DATE]
[CASE SHORT NAME] – TODAY: review and open response workflow for [DISCOVERY TYPE], Set [SET NUMBER]
Responses to Special Interrogatories when present
Responses to RFP and production index when present
Final discovery-service transmittal email — exact wording to be standardized.
Retrieve served papers, proof, Master Memo, approved samples, current case data, service list, and protective order.
Link every draft, verification, and proof to due-date package.
Paralegal verifies sets, service data, and calculations.
Attorney approves objections and final responses.

records.trigger: A physical document, client drop-off, signed original, or other hard-copy item is scanned or uploaded.
records.rule_procedure:
JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Scan, save to correct folder, classify, and route to a specific trigger.
• Manual fallback remains required until automation is tested.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• Is the scan only a channel, or does the document contain a specific trigger?
• Must the original be retained?
• Is OCR reliable?

records.trigger: The firm files, serves, sends, or signs its own work product and that act creates a deadline or next step.
records.rule_procedure:
JACKLAW PROCEDURE
• Outgoing work product is a first-class trigger, not merely an output.
• Create opponent-response and internal follow-up package.
